data.title: TriMas Maintained at Buy as Analyst Reaffirms $45 Price Target on Strong Balance Sheet and Earnings Power
data.description:
BWS Financial analyst Hamed Khorsand has maintained their bullish stance on TRS stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Hamed Khorsand has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including TriMas’s strong balance sheet and earnings power. The company exited the first quarter of 2026 with a substantial net cash position and has two business units that are expected to each contribute meaningfully to adjusted EBITDA by 2027, underscoring a solid long‑term earnings profile.

TriMas also exceeded his revenue expectations in the latest quarter, with part of the upside tied to tooling sales that should translate into future project revenue, reinforcing forward visibility. In addition, shares currently trade at an enterprise value that appears low relative to the company’s projected adjusted EBITDA and anticipated cost-driven margin improvements, while ongoing share repurchases and potential strategic interest from acquirers further support his unchanged $45 price target and Buy view.

data.title: Parsons: Strong Backlog and Bookings Support Multi-Year Growth; Buy Rating Reiterated with Unchanged $72 Price Target
data.description:
TD Cowen analyst Gautam Khanna has maintained their bullish stance on PSN stock, giving a Buy rating yesterday.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Gautam Khanna has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Parsons’ operating momentum and backlog strength. The company delivered first-quarter earnings meaningfully above consensus on better-than-expected margins, while maintaining its 2026 sales and adjusted EBITDA targets, which implies a robust second-half ramp supported by key programs and a clearer revenue cadence.

He also highlights particularly strong bookings in both Federal Solutions and Critical Infrastructure, solid growth and visibility in Middle East operations, and expanding opportunities from FAA modernization work that should increasingly support revenue through 2026. Taken together, these drivers underpin confidence in multi‑year growth, justify keeping the price target unchanged at $72, and support a continued Buy recommendation on the shares.

In another report released yesterday, Stifel Nicolaus also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $79.00 price target.

data.title: Sixt SE Initiated at Buy on Improving Rental Car Industry Pricing, Utilization, and Earnings Outlook
data.description:
Jefferies analyst Constantin Hesse has maintained their bullish stance on 0NW7 stock, giving a Buy rating on April 16.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Constantin Hesse has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that signal a healthier environment for the rental car industry, which directly benefits Sixt SE. Avis’ first‑quarter performance indicates tighter fleet management, improved utilization, and a clear shift toward price discipline rather than pure volume growth, all of which support more sustainable profitability across the sector.

In the U.S., resilient demand, better visibility into the key summer season, and the first notable pricing upturn since 2022 point to firmer fundamentals, while international trends show stronger intra‑European travel and structurally higher revenue per day as fleets are steered toward more profitable segments. With industry players prioritizing returns on assets and maintaining record utilization despite smaller fleets, Hesse expects Sixt’s premium brand, strong utilization focus, and pricing power to translate these favorable market dynamics into outsized earnings potential, justifying a Buy recommendation.

According to TipRanks, Hesse is ranked #1244 out of 12160 analysts.

In another report released on April 16, Deutsche Bank also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a €85.00 price target.

data.title: Flowserve: Buy Rating Reiterated as Analyst Sees Temporary Headwinds and Trims Price Target to $85 on Delayed Orders
data.description:
Analyst Joseph C Giordano from TD Cowen maintained a Buy rating on Flowserve and decreased the price target to $85.00 from $100.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Joseph C Giordano has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including his view that the current Middle East–related disruption is a temporary setback that ultimately should translate into a stronger, multi‑year demand environment for Flowserve. While he acknowledges near‑term execution and communication missteps that have put pressure on the shares, he notes that management is already seeing normalization in key North American maintenance and repair activity, supporting the case that recent softness is transitory.

Giordano also emphasizes that his longer‑term earnings projections through FY27 are only modestly adjusted, reflecting a slight delay in order timing rather than a deterioration in the fundamental outlook. He maintains that the company is well positioned to benefit from an improved order environment in 2027 and 2028, and his revised $85 price target, based on mid‑ to high‑teens forward P/E multiples, still offers enough upside to justify a Buy rating despite the nearer‑term uncertainty and investor caution.

In another report released today, Robert W. Baird also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $91.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on JFrog, Cites Strong Growth Outlook and Competitive Moat; Price Target Maintained at $78
data.description:
Analyst Shrenik Kothari of Robert W. Baird maintained a Buy rating on JFrog, retaining the price target of $78.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shrenik Kothari has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to JFrog’s growth outlook and risk‑reward profile. He sees Q1 and FY26 set up favorably, with achievable cloud growth targets, a history of outperformance even if beat magnitude moderates, and a model where committed cloud contracts limit downside while incremental usage, security attach, and large migrations provide upside. Valuation is viewed as reasonable relative to the company’s consistent execution and its medium‑term margin and free‑cash‑flow targets, supporting a constructive stance.

Kothari also emphasizes JFrog’s competitive moat in Artifactory and its role as an independent control plane for software binaries, which becomes more important as AI‑driven coding expands binary creation and movement. He highlights strong customer and partner feedback on the platform, early but growing traction in security (especially Curation) amid rising supply‑chain and open‑source attacks, and believes these dynamics should sustain robust cloud demand and reinforce the company’s long‑term strategic position. The price target is unchanged at $78 (prior $78).

In another report released yesterday, BTIG also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $60.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on Federal Realty After Strong Q1, Raised 2026 FFO Guidance and Robust Leasing Momentum
data.description:
Analyst Juan C. Sanabria of BMO Capital maintained a Buy rating on Federal Realty, with a price target of $120.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Juan C. Sanabria has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Federal Realty’s stronger‑than‑expected first‑quarter performance, driven by higher occupancy, term fees, and cost efficiencies. He also highlights robust leasing momentum with double‑digit rent spreads, which is translating into healthy same‑store net operating income growth and supports a favorable multi‑year earnings outlook.

Additionally, Sanabria notes that management raised 2026 FFO guidance above prior levels and Street expectations, even after factoring in a brief dip before recently signed leases begin contributing later in 2026 and into 2027. He believes that redevelopment and capital recycling are adding accretive growth on top of solid underlying fundamentals, and that the company’s high‑quality demographic footprint should prove defensive and attractive amid broader macroeconomic uncertainty.

data.title: Five9 Earns Buy Rating on Surging AI Subscription Growth, Strong CCaaS Momentum and Enhanced Transparency
data.description:
William Blair analyst Arjun Bhatia has reiterated their bullish stance on FIVN stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Arjun Bhatia has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including a stronger-than-expected quarter in which subscription revenue growth ticked higher, supported by solid conversion of backlog. He also views the rapid acceleration in AI subscription revenue, now a meaningful share of total subscriptions, as evidence that Five9 is successfully capturing rising demand for AI-driven contact center solutions.

In addition, Bhatia highlights management’s new, more detailed disclosures, which enhance transparency around AI and core CCaaS trends and should help investors better track performance. He also notes that core CCaaS revenue growth remains steady, underpinned by healthy seat expansion, robust upsell and cross-sell activity, and a long runway as on-premises contact centers migrate to the cloud and increasingly adopt AI.
